bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s first offer is valid but gives me low utility, and their message is only a general appeal rather than evidence of constraints or concession patterns.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent’s latest offer actually gives me slightly lower utility than their opening offer, so there is no evidence of meaningful concession yet. I am targeting a near-maximum-utility bid while making only a tiny concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my previous exact bid; the opponent’s emotional messages are noted but not treated as binding evidence.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on a lower-weight issue. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, though their latest offer improved slightly via issueC; their emotional messages are cheap talk, so I am relying on the bid history rather than the claims of fairness.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.83 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent’s offers remain far below an acceptable level for me, despite a small movement on issueC. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual offer history shows they are mostly holding firm on valueE; I will make a modest concession on low-weight issueD while protecting my most important issues issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making only small concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low utility to me and show limited/uneven concession, while their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than verifiable movement. I will hold firm on my key issues A, B, and C, and vary low-weight issueD/issueE to avoid repeating my prior bid exactly.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on my lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and mostly push valueE/valueD, so I am holding firm on high-importance issueA, issueB, and issueC while conceding issueD and issueE toward values they have repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.76 because it is early in the negotiation (t=0.12), so I should not concede on my most important issues yet. The opponent’s offers have improved mainly on issueC, now matching my top value there, while their messages are cooperative but not binding; I will reciprocate by keeping issueC at valueB and offering concessions on lower-weight issueD and issueE.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions mainly on lower-weight issues rather than giving up issueA, issueB, or issueC. The opponent’s offers remain mostly anchored on valueE and only show limited movement, with one notable concession on issueC, so I will hold firm on my most important values and offer flexibility on issueD/issueE.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making limited concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly push valueE/valueD, with only small movement on issueC, so I will hold firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C while varying concessions on D/E to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a strong utility of about 0.77 because it is still early at t=0.18, so I should protect my high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding on lower-weight D and E. The opponent’s offers show limited real concession, though they have twice offered issueC: valueB, while their emotional messages do not change the numerical assessment.

I am targeting a strong utility bid around 0.75 while conceding mainly on lower-weight issues D and E, since it is still early at t=0.20 and I should hold firm on high-weight issueA, issueB, and issueC. The opponent’s offers remain quite low for me, though they have occasionally moved on issueC and recently used issueD/valueE and issueE/valueD, so I am matching those signals where the cost to me is modest.

At t=0.22 we are still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for us while conceding mainly on lower-weight issues D and E. The opponent’s offers remain very poor for us on high-weight issueA and issueB, though they have occasionally moved on issueC, so I will hold firm on A/B/C and offer flexibility on the less important issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.75, which is a meaningful concession from my maximum while still protecting the high-weight issuesA, issueB, and issueC this early in the negotiation. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the bids: they have repeatedly offered issueC: valueB and seem to care about concessions on issueD/issueE, so I concede those lower-weight issues while holding firm on my core priorities.

At t=0.26 we are still early enough to protect my high-weight issues, so I am targeting a strong utility around 0.76 while conceding mainly on low-weight issueD and issueE. The opponent’s offers remain poor for me on issueA and issueB, but they have sometimes moved on issueC to my best value, so I will hold issueA/issueB/issueC and vary the low-priority concessions rather than repeat my last bid exactly.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 while making a meaningful concession because we are past the opening but not yet near the deadline. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueB/issueD/issueE toward valueE and only recently conceded issueA to my top value, so I will hold firm on high-value issueA and issueC while conceding lower-priority issueD/issueE and partially on issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69 because we are only at t=0.30, so I should concede selectively without giving up my most important issues. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall despite emotional cooperation messages, but they have shown some movement on issueA and issueC; I will hold those high-value positions while conceding more on lower-weight issueD and issueE and partially on issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69, holding firm on high-value issueA and issueC while conceding on lower-weight issueD and partly on issueB where the opponent has recently moved. At t=0.32 there is some need to show progress, but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s much lower-utility bid; their messages are cooperative but the real evidence is gradual concessions on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 because we are only about one-third through the negotiation, so I should concede selectively rather than move to the opponent’s latest low-value package. The opponent’s offers show some real movement on issueA, issueB, and issueC, but they continue to push weak values for my lower-weight issueD and issueE; their emotional messages are noted but I rely mainly on the bid pattern. I will hold firm on my highest-value issueA and issueC while offering concessions on lower-priority issues and a moderate concession on issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.67, since time is only at 0.36 and there is not yet severe deadline pressure. The opponent’s offers show some real movement on issueA and issueC, but they remain tough on issueB and low-weight issues, so I am holding my most important issueA and issueC while conceding issueD and issueE toward values they have recently proposed. Their emotional messages are noted but not treated as binding; the actual offer pattern matters more.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69, since we are still in the mid-game and should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s recent offers show real movement on my high-value issueA and issueC, but they continue to push hard on issueB and the low-weight issueD/issueE; I am holding issueA and issueC while conceding issueD and issueE and offering issueB:valueB, which they have previously proposed.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.66, conceding mainly on lower-priority or recently contested issues while holding my strongest priorities on issueA and issueC. The opponent’s offers have gradually improved on issueA and issueC at times, but they still mostly press for concessions on issueB/issueD/issueE; their emotional messages are noted but I am relying on the bid history rather than the appeals.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.71, conceding mainly on lower-weight or partially flexible issues while holding firm on issueA and issueC, which are more valuable to me. The opponent’s offers have improved at times on issueA and issueC, including prior willingness to offer issueA:valueA and issueC:valueB, while their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than binding constraints. At t=0.42 there is still time, so I will make a meaningful but not excessive concession by accepting their recent movement on issueB and issueD/E tradeoffs.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.704 while making mid-game concessions on lower-weight issuesD and issueE and accepting the opponent’s recent movement on issueB. The opponent’s offers have gradually become better for me, including recent willingness to use issueA:valueA, issueB:valueB, and issueC:valueB at various points, while their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than binding constraints.
